notes:
Seti Astro Suite Pro

V1.18.0
SSSC — Spectrophotometric Standard Star Calibration
SSSC is a next-generation empirical color calibration that replaces the sensor QE curve assumption entirely. Instead of trusting manufacturer datasheets, it solves for your imaging system's true effective throughput R(λ) directly from Gaia XP stellar spectra — the same mathematical framework Gaia uses to self-calibrate internally.
Three bootstrap stages activate automatically based on your field:

Stage 1 (5+ stars): scalar per-channel gains — equivalent to traditional SPCC
Stage 2 (50+ stars): color-dependent band response, no QE assumption
Stage 3 (200+ stars, B-V span ≥ 1.5): full per-channel R(λ) curve solved via coordinate descent on piecewise-linear control points

Each run refines the solution and seeds the next session. The Camera/Rig label field keeps session history separate per imaging train.
Works with any filter set — dedicated astro filters, Bayer+QE combined curves for OSC, LP/cut filters in the optical path. Whatever your filter curves don't capture, R(λ) absorbs empirically from the stars themselves. Substantially less sensitive to input curve accuracy than traditional SPCC.

Nebula Flythrough — New tool for creating cinematic zoom videos from your astrophotos.
Composite a stars-only image over a starless image using Screen blend, with independent zoom center and zoom rate per layer, to produce the effect of flying into a nebula along a curved trajectory. Exports MP4 video via OpenCV, with GPU acceleration via CUDA or MPS where available.
Per-layer effects include:
Nebula depth warp — luminance-driven per-pixel parallax (bright regions zoom in faster, zero banding)
Radial edge stretch — rubber-sheet distortion toward the edges
Zoom blur — radial warp-speed star streaks
Chromatic aberration — R/B channel split for a cinematic lens feel

All effects can be ramped with zoom velocity so they build naturally as the flythrough accelerates. A live scrubber and play preview let you dial in the look before committing to a full render. Working resolution presets (SD through 4K) keep preview snappy while export uses full-resolution source images.
